Badger Grapple Red — v21.0 Trainer Sight-Lines

The first of two structural gameplay items identified as bigger moves
toward FireRed comparability than another art pass (the other being
discrete building interiors, which is a much larger undertaking and
deliberately not attempted in this pass).

WHAT CHANGED: Campus Quad's two real trainers (Buckshot, Rex) now use
FireRed's classic sentry convention — a fixed position, a facing
direction, and a straight-line sight range (5 tiles) that auto-triggers
a battle the moment you walk into it, with a "!" alert and a distinct
"spotted" line different from their approach-triggered dialogue. Walking
up and pressing A still works too, for approaching from outside their
sight cone. Line-of-sight respects collision (an obstacle between you
and the trainer blocks detection, same as a real wall would).

This required making the two trainers stationary — they previously
patrolled a small 4-tile loop, which would have meant their facing
direction (and therefore sight cone) changed constantly. Kept patrol
routes on the three flavor-only NPCs (Scout, Manager, Student) since
those aren't battle triggers and the ambiguity doesn't matter for them.

VERIFICATION — this one actually caught two of my own test mistakes
before shipping, worth being honest about:
1. First full click-driven test succeeded (sightLocked became true at
   the exact right tile, transitioned cleanly to BattleScene).
2. A follow-up test with slightly different click timing appeared to
   fail — looked like a real bug. It wasn't: it was click-cadence
   flakiness against the existing 95ms input throttle, confirmed by
   re-running the exact same movement sequence with explicit per-step
   state logging, which reproduced the correct trigger deterministically
   at (20,9). Chasing a phantom bug here would have wasted time fixing
   test flakiness as if it were product flakiness.
2. To get a clean, timing-independent read on the actual logic (spot
   correctly / correctly skip when already defeated), drove scene state
   directly rather than trusting click timing: confirmed sightLocked
   becomes true against an undefeated trainer and stays false against
   a defeated one, in the same session, isolating the real behavior
   from any movement-animation timing.
3. Confirmed the manual walk-up-and-press-A fallback still works,
   approaching from an angle outside the sight cone.
Zero console errors across every run.

Cache: ?v=210. Proof: title/note reads "v21.0 Trainer Sight-Lines".
